source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to 22" Casing Connection

actions_taken: Investigation committee formed by operator on January 19, 2024
BSEE onsite investigation conducted on February 7, 2024
Metallurgical testing of casing connections performed at materials lab in Houston
Immediate preservation order issued for all equipment and casing related to incident
event.summary: On January 18, 2024, during drilling operations at GC 478, OCS-G35662, Well 004 on the drillship Noble Stanley Lafosse, a 22" casing connection failed at the rig floor. The S-90/MT pin connection of joint 16 was cross-threaded and made up only ¼ - 3/8 turn instead of the required ½ - ¾ turn. When the flush-mounted slips holding joint 17 were opened to install anti-rotation keys, the axial load transferred to the improperly made-up connection, causing the pin to separate from the box. Seventy-two joints of casing fell partially into the hole and onto the seafloor, breaking apart and impacting critical subsea infrastructure. No injuries or environmental impacts occurred, but damages exceeded $15 million.
recommendations: Ensure visual inspection of casing connections is completed as per approved running procedures to detect cross-threading and makeup discrepancies
Clarify in JSA and running procedures when anti-rotation keys should be installed relative to slip release
Implement supervisor verification of connection makeup before slips are released
Establish clear communication protocols between crew members regarding casing positioning and connection verification

threats.name: Cross-threading of casing connection during makeup
Inadequate makeup turn depth (¼ - 3/8 turn vs. ½ - ¾ turn required)
Premature release of slips before anti-rotation keys installed
Failure to visually inspect connection for defects

consequences.name: Casing string parting at rig floor
72 joints of casing falling into hole and onto seafloor
Impact damage to critical subsea infrastructure

controls.name: Casing makeup procedure specifying ½ - ¾ turn requirement
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure proper connection engagement

supporting_text: The casing only making up ¼ - 3/8 turn instead of ½ - ¾ turn as specified by the contractor's procedures for proper make up
A review of still pictures from the rig video indicated that the connection rotated only ¼ - 3/8 turn during the makeup, instead of the ½ - ¾ turn specified in the contractor's procedure

controls.name: Visual inspection of casing connection makeup by contractor's onsite representative
controls.barrier_role: Detection and verification of proper connection engagement

supporting_text: the contractor's rig-site rep did not complete a visual inspection of the makeup as per the approved contractor's running procedure, missing an approximately 0.143" gap between the pin indicator and box connection

The contractor's onsite representative failed to identify this discrepancy. They also did not complete a visual inspection of makeup as per the approved Contractor's running procedure and missed an approximately 0.143" gap between the pin indicator and the box connection
If a visual inspection of the connection was performed the evidence of cross threading could have been noticed

controls.name: Flush-mounted slips holding casing joint during makeup and lowering
controls.barrier_role: Mechanical support to prevent axial load transfer to connection during anti-rotation key installation

supporting_text: The flush-mounted slips holding joint 17 were temporary opened to lower the connection to install two vertical anti-rotation keys in the connection. When the slips opened, the axial load of the 22" was transferred to the connection, the pin separated from the box, resulting in the casing parting at the rig floor

controls.name: JSA and running procedure specifying when to install anti-rotation keys
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure anti-rotation keys are installed before slips are released
controls.lod_basis: Job Safety Analysis and contractor running procedure

supporting_text: A change in the work process was observed after a crew change when casing joint 51 was made up in the string. The rotary slips were temporarily opened and the connection was lowered to facilitate the installation of the anti-rotation keys. Anti-rotation keys on previous connections were installed without lowering the connection. After looking through the JSA and the Contractor's running procedure we could not find supporting documents showing if the anti-rotation keys are to be installed before the slips are opened

the JSA and running procedure failing to identify when to install the anti-rotation keys. This led to the slips being released before the anti-rotation keys were installed, this took away another chance to verify the connection was made up properly

controls.name: Supervisor verification of connection makeup before slips release
controls.barrier_role: Supervisory oversight to ensure proper connection engagement and prevent premature slip release

supporting_text: Supervision – The contractor's trainee signal for the casing to be repositioned in the slips without the Contractor's supervisor verifying the connection
A contributing cause to this incident was the Contractor trainee signaling for the casing to be repositioned without the Contractor supervisor verifying the connection is made up properly

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting (PJSM) for casing running operations
controls.barrier_role: Communication and awareness of hazards and procedures before work begins

supporting_text: On January 17, 2024, at 04:00 hours, a pre-job safety meeting (PJSM) is held with crews on running 22" casing

controls.name: Rotating Link Adapter repair and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Equipment maintenance to ensure proper functioning of top drive system

supporting_text: Casing operations start at 04:30 but are shut down to the repair the Rotating Link Adapter on the top drive

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
